root_text.an8.42:1.1: “Aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato, bhikkhave, uposatho upavuttho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an8.42:1.2: Kathaṁ upavuttho ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro?
root_text.an8.42:1.4: ‘yāvajīvaṁ arahanto pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā nihitadaṇḍā nihitasatthā lajjī dayāpannā, sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampino viharanti.
root_text.an8.42:1.5: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho lajjī dayāpanno, sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharāmi.
root_text.an8.42:1.6: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.42:2.1: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭiviratā nīcaseyyaṁ kappenti—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
root_text.an8.42:2.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭivirato nīcaseyyaṁ kappemi—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
root_text.an8.42:2.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.42:2.5: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an8.42:3.2: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, yo imesaṁ soḷasannaṁ mahājanapadānaṁ pahūtarattaratanānaṁ issariyādhipaccaṁ rajjaṁ kāreyya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an8.42:3.3: aṅgānaṁ magadhānaṁ kāsīnaṁ kosalānaṁ vajjīnaṁ mallānaṁ cetīnaṁ vaṅgānaṁ kurūnaṁ pañcālānaṁ macchānaṁ sūrasenānaṁ assakānaṁ avantīnaṁ gandhārānaṁ kambojānaṁ, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgatassa uposathassa etaṁ kalaṁ nāgghati soḷasiṁ.
root_text.an8.42:3.5: Kapaṇaṁ, bhikkhave, mānusakaṁ rajjaṁ dibbaṁ sukhaṁ upanidhāya.
root_text.an8.42:4.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni paññāsa vassāni, cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:4.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni pañca vassasatāni cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:4.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:4.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:5.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni vassasatāni, tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:5.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbaṁ vassasahassaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:5.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā tāvatiṁsānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:5.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:6.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni dve vassasatāni, yāmānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:6.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni dve vassasahassāni yāmānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:6.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā yāmānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:6.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:7.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni cattāri vassasatāni, tusitānaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:7.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni cattāri vassasahassāni tusitānaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:7.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā tusitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:7.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:8.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni aṭṭha vassasatāni, nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:8.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni aṭṭha vassasahassāni nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:8.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā nimmānaratīnaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:8.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:9.1: Yāni, bhikkhave, mānusakāni soḷasa vassasatāni, paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ eso eko rattindivo.
root_text.an8.42:9.4: Tena saṁvaccharena dibbāni soḷasa vassasahassāni paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an8.42:9.5: Ṭhānaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, vijjati yaṁ idhekacco itthī vā puriso vā aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgataṁ uposathaṁ upavasitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā paranimmitavasavattīnaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.an8.42:9.6: Idaṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhave, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.an8.42:10.2: Musā na bhāse na ca majjapo siyā;
root_text.an8.42:11.1: Mālaṁ na dhāre na ca gandhamācare,
root_text.an8.42:12.1: Cando ca suriyo ca ubho sudassanā,
root_text.an8.42:15.1: Tasmā hi nārī ca naro ca sīlavā,

translation_text.an8.42:0.3: The Sabbath With Eight Factors, In Detail
translation_text.an8.42:1.1: “Mendicants, the observance of the sabbath with its eight factors is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.
translation_text.an8.42:1.2: And how should it be observed?
translation_text.an8.42:1.3: It’s when a noble disciple reflects:
translation_text.an8.42:1.4: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. They are scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an8.42:1.5: I, too, for this day and night will give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. I’ll be scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an8.42:1.6: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.42:2.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up high and luxurious beds. They sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an8.42:2.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up high and luxurious beds. I’ll sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an8.42:2.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.42:2.5: The observance of the sabbath with its eight factors in this way is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.
translation_text.an8.42:3.2: Suppose you were to rule as sovereign lord over these sixteen great countries—Aṅga, Magadha, Kāsi, Kosala, Vajji, Malla, Cetī, Vaccha, Kuru, Pañcāla, Maccha, Sūrasena, Assaka, Avanti, Gandhāra, and Kamboja—full of the seven kinds of precious things.
translation_text.an8.42:3.3: This wouldn’t be worth a sixteenth part of the sabbath with its eight factors.
translation_text.an8.42:3.5: Because human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.
translation_text.an8.42:4.1: Fifty years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of the four great kings.
translation_text.an8.42:4.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:4.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:4.4: The lifespan of the gods of the four great kings is five hundred of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:4.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of the four great kings.
translation_text.an8.42:4.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:4.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:5.1: A hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.an8.42:5.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:5.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:5.4: The lifespan of the gods of the thirty-three is a thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:5.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.an8.42:5.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:5.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:6.1: Two hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods of Yama.
translation_text.an8.42:6.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:6.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:6.4: The lifespan of the gods of Yama is two thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:6.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods of Yama.
translation_text.an8.42:6.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:6.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:7.1: Four hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the joyful gods.
translation_text.an8.42:7.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:7.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:7.4: The lifespan of the joyful gods is four thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:7.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the joyful gods.
translation_text.an8.42:7.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:7.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:8.1: Eight hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods who love to create.
translation_text.an8.42:8.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:8.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:8.4: The life span of the gods who love to create is eight thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:8.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods who love to create.
translation_text.an8.42:8.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:8.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:9.1: Sixteen hundred years in the human realm is one day and night for the gods who control what is created by others.
translation_text.an8.42:9.2: Thirty such days make up a month.
translation_text.an8.42:9.3: Twelve such months make up a year.
translation_text.an8.42:9.4: The life span of the gods who control what is created by others is sixteen thousand of these heavenly years.
translation_text.an8.42:9.5: It’s possible that some woman or man who has observed the eight-factored sabbath will—when their body breaks up, after death—be reborn in the company of the gods who control what is created by others.
translation_text.an8.42:9.6: This is what I was referring to when I said:
translation_text.an8.42:9.7: ‘Human kingship is a poor thing compared to the happiness of the gods.’
translation_text.an8.42:10.1: You shouldn’t kill living creatures, or steal,
translation_text.an8.42:10.4: and don’t eat at night, the wrong time.
translation_text.an8.42:11.1: Not wearing garlands or applying fragrance,
translation_text.an8.42:11.2: you should sleep on a low bed, <j>or a mat on the ground.
translation_text.an8.42:11.3: This is the eight-factored sabbath, they say,
translation_text.an8.42:11.4: explained by the Buddha, <j>who has gone to suffering’s end.
translation_text.an8.42:12.1: The moon and sun are both fair to see,
translation_text.an8.42:12.2: radiating as far as they revolve.
translation_text.an8.42:12.3: Those dispellers of darkness, travellers in midair,
translation_text.an8.42:12.4: shine in the firmament, lighting up the quarters.
translation_text.an8.42:13.1: All of the wealth that’s found in this realm—
translation_text.an8.42:14.1: they’re not worth a sixteenth part
translation_text.an8.42:14.2: of the sabbath with its eight factors,
translation_text.an8.42:14.3: as starlight cannot rival the moon.
translation_text.an8.42:15.1: So an ethical woman or man,
translation_text.an8.42:15.2: who has observed the eight-factored sabbath,
translation_text.an8.42:15.3: having made merit whose outcome is happiness,
translation_text.an8.42:15.4: blameless, they go to a heavenly place.”
